title: A mutating Codex session can work deep into the primary checkout without being refused
description: The session created a feature branch in the primary checkout and reached the landing gate before a human noticed it was not isolated.
statement: A change/build session starting in the primary checkout has no fail-fast boundary requiring a managed worktree before it edits.
evidence: This session began at `C:\code\storytree`, ran `git switch -c codex/forest-real-test-inventory`, implemented and visually verified the forest-parcels change, and completed a full gate before the owner asked whether it was in a worktree. `git worktree list --porcelain` then showed `worktree C:/code/storytree` for that branch, not a `.claude/worktrees/*` slot. Moving it later to `.claude/worktrees/forest-real-test-inventory` caused `check:declared` to fail with `session "forest-real-test-inventory" holds NO live claim` until the new worktree identity declared `forest-world`; the primary-path run had not surfaced that boundary.
impact: The feature work shared the primary checkout with an unrelated untracked ADR-0235 edit, creating overwrite/staging risk and requiring a preservation copy, selective commit, branch move, primary fast-forward, user-edit restoration, rebase, dependency provision, and two additional gates. The next Codex session that accepts a primary checkout can repeat the same contamination without noticing until late ceremony.
